channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South African Dancers Triumph at Disney World, Claiming World Championship Titles
item.description: Two 14-year-old dancers from Knysna, Fiah-Bella Fredericks and Chioma Tagbo, have made history by becoming world champions at the Dance the World event in Walt Disney World. Selected through the Theat...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli leads Belgian Grand Prix practice as George Russell struggles with tyre issues
item.description: In the second practice session for the Belgian Grand Prix at Spa-Francorchamps, Kimi Antonelli secured the fastest lap, while his Mercedes teammate George Russell finished eighth, raising concerns abo...

item.title: E Rock criticises musicians cancelling gigs amid backlash, bakery owner condemns ITV The Voice staff for seeking free food
item.description: In a recent incident at the Super Bowl show, E Rock has publicly criticised musicians who have pulled out of their scheduled performances due to backlash. The artist's comments highlight the frustrati...

item.title: Labor's Shift Toward Wealth Taxes: A Decade of Evolution
item.description: The article discusses the Australian Labor Party's evolving stance on wealth taxation, highlighting how the party has moved away from its historical socialist roots. A decade after attempts to distanc...

item.title: Delhi HC denies interim bail to Al Falah chairman but allows limited custody parole for wife's medical needs
item.description: The Delhi High Court has denied interim bail to Jawad Ahmed Siddiqui, chairman of Al Falah University, who is facing two money laundering cases. Siddiqui sought bail citing his wife's critical health ...

item.title: Civic movement criticises prophet's assassination threat against leader
item.description: The civic movement March and March has condemned a prophecy by Zimbabwean prophet Dr Obey Tichafa Mukanhairi, who claimed civic movement leader Jacinta Ngobese-Zuma would be assassinated if she did no...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital Funds Raise Over Sh2.2 Billion for Kidney and Bone Marrow Transplants
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital has successfully raised over Sh2.2 billion through a fundraising gala organized by the Women's Wing of Chama Cha Mapinduzi (UWT) to support life-saving kidney treatments and bo...

item.title: Sigabo: Gabo nahanap ang nawawalang motor dahil sa diskarte ni Tekla
item.description: Ang artikulong ito tungkol sa episode 20 ng palabas na Sigabo, kung saan ang karakter na Gabo ay nahanap ang nawawalang motor sa pamamagitan ng diskarte ni Tekla. Ang mga tauhan na nangunguna sa kuwen...

item.title: Protecting Pets from Wildfire Smoke: Essential Tips for Pet Owners
item.description: As wildfire smoke from Canada spreads across the northern United States, pet owners need to take proactive measures to safeguard their animals' health. The article outlines five critical strategies to...

item.title: 64-Year-Old Man Completes Nursing Degree After Decade in Healthcare
item.description: John Adesoye, a Nigerian-born man who moved to the UK in 2013, has completed his nursing degree at age 64, defying societal norms. Born and raised in Nigeria, he worked as an electronics technician in...

item.title: PM's aide calls for Fazl to retract remarks against martyrs
item.description: PM's political adviser Rana Sanaullah criticized Maulana Fazlur Rehman's remarks about martyrs, calling them 'inappropriate' and suggesting they were made in a moment of anger rather than as part of a...

item.title: Lagos Flooding Crisis: Land Reclamation Threatens Lagoon's Drainage System
item.description: Bode George, former Deputy National Chairman of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), has raised alarm over excessive land reclamation in Lagos State, which he claims has severely reduced the lagoon's c...

item.title: Quebec Man Convicted of Four Murders to Face New Trial After 33 Years in Prison
item.description: Canada's justice minister has ordered a new trial for Daniel Jolivet, a Quebec man convicted of four murders in 1994, after concerns emerged about the fairness of his initial trial. Jolivet, who spent...

item.title: FCC Commissioner Defends Broadcasters After Trump Demands License Revocation
item.description: President Donald Trump faced backlash from FCC Commissioner Anna Gomez after he demanded the Federal Communications Commission revoke the broadcast licenses of ABC and NBC for refusing to air his prim...

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies: Voter List Exclusion Doesn't Automatically Result in Citizenship Loss
item.description: The Supreme Court recently emphasized that being excluded from the voter list during the special intensive revision of electoral rolls in West Bengal does not automatically lead to the loss of citizen...

item.title: ODM Leader Calls for Peace After DCP's By-Election Victory, Defends IEBC
item.description: ODM leader Oburu Oginga has criticized former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua's confrontational approach, urging him to promote peace after DCP's victory in the Ol Kalou by-election. Oginga praised ...

item.title: Tanzania boosts rural water access to 85%
item.description: The article highlights progress in expanding clean water access across Tanzania's rural areas, with coverage now reaching 85% of the population. This development comes amid ongoing challenges in infra...

item.title: Distillery Owner Faces Charges Over 2024 Methanol Poisonings That Killed Australians
item.description: A Laotian distillery owner faces charges over the November 2024 methanol poisoning incident that killed six foreign nationals, including two Australian teenagers. Danish officials claim the defendant ...

item.title: Enugu Cooking Gas Price Drops: Residents Welcome Relief
item.description: The price of Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), commonly known as cooking gas, has significantly dropped in Enugu, according to a market survey. Owners of refilling depots reported that prices fell from N...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Expected to Linger and Spread This Weekend, Affecting Major Cities
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canada continued to impact the United States on July 17, 2026, with Detroit, Chicago, and Washington, D.C. ranking among the world's most polluted major cities. Air quality measure...
